iran_nuke.notes: Day 15 05:00 — FALLING STABLE. No new nuclear signals. Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed to keep blocking Hormuz but no nuclear escalation rhetoric. US strikes on Kharg Island targeted military assets only, explicitly sparing oil infrastructure — calibrated to avoid triggering nuclear desperation. Conventional war intensifying but nuclear track continues de-escalation. Iran rejected Putin's uranium transfer proposal. 15→14 (coupling +4 from Iran conventional imminent, but falling trend).

iran_conventional.notes: Day 15 05:00 — IMMINENT RISING. MAJOR ESCALATIONS: (1) US bombs Kharg Island — Trump says 'obliterated' military targets at Iran's main oil export hub (90% of oil exports). Struck missile storage, mines, army defenses. 15+ explosions. Explicitly spared oil infrastructure BUT threatened to hit it next — Iran parliament speaker had warned this triggers 'new level of retaliation.' (2) 6 US service members killed when KC-135 refueling plane crashed in western Iraq (CNN). Combined with 5 KC-135s damaged at Prince Sultan Air Base = devastating US air asset losses. (3) Iran's Armed Forces threaten to destroy US-linked oil infrastructure if Iran's energy facilities attacked — first explicit threat targeting US commercial energy interests. (4) White House internal tug-of-war over war exit strategy — aides vie to affect outcome as exit remains elusive (Reuters). (5) Brent above $100/bbl for second day, touched $120 earlier. Iran fires 500+ missiles and ~2000 drones since Feb 28. Total Iran casualties: 1,444 killed, 18,551 injured. 100 capped.

israel_lebanon.notes: Day 15 05:00 — IMMINENT RISING. MAJOR ESCALATION: (1) Israel orders sweeping displacement: all residents within 25 miles of Lebanese border told to head north — ~1 million displaced (Guardian). (2) Israel destroys bridge in Lebanon, signals long campaign (Al Jazeera/Reuters). (3) Israel strikes hit heart of Beirut — multiple neighborhoods targeted. (4) 773+ dead in Lebanon. (5) Axios confirms Israel planning massive ground invasion to seize entire area south of Litani River — 'largest since 2006.' Senior official: 'We are going to do what we did in Gaza.' (6) 2 IDF soldiers killed, 14 wounded — first significant casualties. 100 capped.

turkey.notes: CRITICAL RISING. Third intercept confirmed (March 13). NATO intercepted Iranian ballistic missile over eastern Mediterranean — third in 10 days. Turkey asks Tehran to clarify. Iran denies targeting Turkey, calls incidents 'possible false flag.' Key: 53+6(coupling from Iran imminent)=59. One more intercept likely pushes to imminent and triggers Article 4 discussions. Incirlik remains active US nuclear base in theater. Critical coupling from Russia adds +8. 53→59.

india.notes: ELEVATED STABLE. No new direct signals. Modi expressed 'deep concern' over escalation, spoke with Iranian President about Indian citizen safety. IndiGo fuel surcharge from oil crisis. UK FCDO advises against travel within 10km of India-Pakistan border and J&K. West Asia crisis creating internal security concerns for India (Pakistan may foment Kashmir unrest). 10+10(elevated zone coupling)=20.

russia.notes: Day 15 05:00 — CRITICAL RISING. (1) Polymarket: Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by end of March drops to 2% — effectively dead. (2) Russia-Ukraine war benefits from oil price shock — high energy prices fill Moscow's war chest (Canadian analysts). (3) ISW: Global oil price shock from Iran war has 'likely invalidated' assumptions about Russian economic downturn forcing Kremlin concessions. (4) US sanctions license operational — Canada maintains separate Russia sanctions. (5) Macron: Russia shouldn't demand Middle East ceasefire while refusing Ukraine ceasefire. 32→38 (coupling: +4 from Iran imminent, +8 from Turkey critical = +12 total).

china.notes: Day 15 05:00 — DETERRENT RISING. MIXED SIGNALS: (1) China warplane flights near Taiwan resume after 7-day lull — 5 PLA aircraft detected, 3 crossed strait median line (Japan Times/AP). Trend still dramatically down from peak of 92. (2) US THAAD missile defense system being relocated from South Korea to Middle East — creates Asia defense gap. China Foreign Ministry reiterates opposition to THAAD deployment. (3) NYT analysis: 'How the War in Iran Could Help China' — US military resources diverted from Indo-Pacific creates strategic window. (4) $14B Taiwan arms deal still awaits Trump approval. China appears to be letting US overextend in Middle East. 14→18 (removed china_reduced_flights, added thaad_redeployment +6, coupling +0).

north_korea.notes: ELEVATED RISING. BREAKING: North Korea launched suspected ballistic missile — Japan government confirms, projectile already fallen (Kyodo News, Mar 14). Second missile test this cycle. KCNA condemns Japan Type-12 missile deployment to Kyushu, calls it 'preparations for future war.' Kim Jong Un + daughter gun practice photos published. DPRK accuses Japan of 'raising regional security risks.' US reportedly exploring talks with DPRK. South Korea PM meets Trump — US and SK militaries discuss possible Patriot redeployment to Middle East. 16→22 (dprk_missile_test reinforced, coupling +2 from China deterrent+6).

russia_ukraine.notes: Day 15 05:00 — CRITICAL RISING. (1) Ceasefire by end of March now 2% on Polymarket — effectively dead. (2) ISW: Oil price shock from Iran war benefits Russia economically, undermines sanctions pressure. (3) Zelensky: US sought postponement of trilateral talks. (4) Sweden boards suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker. (5) Ukrainian GUR strikes Russian radar stations and drone infrastructure. (6) France pledges continued Ukraine support: 'Russia is mistaken, hoping for a respite.' 35→45 (coupling: +5 from Russia critical +5 from Turkey critical).

pakistan_afghanistan.notes: CRITICAL RISING. ESCALATION: (1) Pakistan airstrikes hit civilian areas in Afghanistan — 6 killed (LA Times). Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of 'targeting civilians.' (2) Ongoing border clashes with casualties on both sides. (3) Saudi-Pakistan Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement active. (4) India condemns Pakistan strikes, supports Afghan sovereignty. (5) Qatar mediating — Qatari FM discussed tension reduction with Pakistan FM. (6) Pakistan-occupied Gilgit-Baltistan protests — anti-government slogans. 24→36 (new signals: pakistan_airstrikes_civilians +8, border_clashes +6, coupling +10 from India critical).

changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: CRITICAL — Day 15 05:00 DAILY SCAN — GLOBAL WAR PROBABILITY SURGES TO 83% (IMMINENT). UP 25 POINTS FROM YESTERDAY (58%). TRIPLE-DIGIT CONFLICT ZONES: Iran conventional (100), Israel-Lebanon (100). MAJOR DEVELOPMENTS: (1) US BOMBS KHARG ISLAND: Trump orders strike on Iran's primary oil export hub. Military targets destroyed, oil infrastructure explicitly spared BUT threatened. Iran warns 'new level of retaliation.' (2) 6 US SOLDIERS KILLED: KC-135 crash in Iraq adds to devastating US air asset losses (5 damaged at Saudi base + 6 killed = catastrophic). (3) ISRAEL ORDERS 25-MILE DISPLACEMENT: ~1 million Lebanese displaced. Massive ground invasion planning confirmed. Heart of Beirut struck. (4) DPRK BALLISTIC MISSILE TEST: Second test confirmed, Japan reports. (5) COUPLING CASCADE: Iran imminent + Turkey critical + Russia critical = system-wide risk amplification pushing global score to 83%. NUCLEAR TRACK: Still de-escalating at 14%. Iran's new Supreme Leader vows Hormuz blockade continuation but no nuclear escalation rhetoric. US deliberately spares oil infrastructure at Kharg to avoid nuclear trigger. WATCH: (1) Iran retaliation for Kharg Island strike — parliament speaker warned 'new level.' (2) Lebanon ground invasion deployment — imminent? (3) 4th Turkey intercept → Article 4 trigger. (4) DPRK follow-up missile — was this a test or provocation? (5) Oil prices as IEA 400M barrel release hits market. (6) US air asset losses — cannot sustain KC-135 attrition rate. (7) Pakistan-Afghanistan escalation adding third active conflict zone in Asia.

latest_news.headline: US bombs Kharg Island — Trump says 'obliterated' military targets at Iran's main oil export hub
latest_news.text: US military conducted large bombing raid on Kharg Island — handles 90% of Iran's oil exports. Trump said forces 'obliterated every military target' but 'chose NOT to wipe out oil infrastructure.' 15+ explosions reported. Iran's parliament speaker had warned this would trigger 'new level of retaliation.' Iran's Armed Forces now threaten to destroy US-linked oil infrastructure if Iran's energy facilities are attacked.

latest_news.headline: 6 US service members killed when KC-135 refueling plane crashes in western Iraq
latest_news.text: Six US service members were killed when their KC-135 refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq. Combined with 5 KC-135s damaged by Iranian missile strike at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia — devastating attrition of US air refueling fleet in theater.

latest_news.headline: Israel orders sweeping displacement — all residents within 25 miles of border told north
latest_news.text: Israel issued sweeping new displacement order for southern Lebanon — instructing all residents within 25 miles of the border to head north. Approximately 1 million people displaced. Combined with strikes on heart of Beirut and confirmed ground invasion planning, signals imminent massive escalation.

latest_news.headline: North Korea launches suspected ballistic missile — Japan government confirms
latest_news.text: North Korea launched a suspected ballistic missile, Japanese Defense Ministry said Saturday. Projectile appears to have already fallen. Second North Korean missile test this cycle, coming amid condemnation of Japan's Type-12 missile deployment and as US considers moving THAAD from South Korea to Middle East.

latest_news.headline: NATO intercepts third Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey in 10 days
latest_news.text: NATO air defences in eastern Mediterranean shot down third ballistic missile fired from Iran toward Turkey. Turkey asked Tehran to clarify. Iran denies targeting Turkey, describes incidents as 'possible false flag attacks.' Third intercept in 10 days — one more likely triggers NATO Article 4 consultations.

latest_news.headline: Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by end of March drops to 2% on Polymarket
latest_news.text: Prediction market probability of Russia-Ukraine ceasefire before March 31 has declined to just 2%. The Iran war has effectively frozen US-mediated peace talks. ISW reports that the global oil price shock from the Iran war is benefiting Russia's economy, undermining sanctions pressure.

latest_news.headline: US THAAD missile defense relocated from South Korea to Middle East
latest_news.text: Pentagon moving THAAD missile defense system from South Korea to Middle East. All six launcher vehicles have left. Creates significant defense gap in Asia. South Korea PM meets Trump — discusses alternative defense arrangements. China reiterates opposition to THAAD in Korea. Analysts warn: 'How the war in Iran could help China.'

latest_news.headline: Pakistan airstrikes kill 6 civilians in Afghanistan — border clashes escalate
latest_news.text: Pakistan airstrikes hit civilian areas in Afghanistan, killing 6 people. Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of 'targeting civilians.' Ongoing border clashes with casualties on both sides. India condemns strikes, supports Afghan sovereignty. Qatar mediating. Saudi-Pakistan defense agreement active.

latest_news.headline: Iran's new Supreme Leader vows to keep blocking Strait of Hormuz
latest_news.text: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei pledged to maintain effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which normally transports about one-fifth of global oil supplies. Brent crude stayed above $100/barrel. Goldman Sachs predicts $100+ average for March.

latest_news.headline: White House internal tug-of-war over Iran war exit strategy
latest_news.text: A complex tug-of-war inside the White House is driving Trump's shifting public statements on the Iran war, as aides debate when and how to declare victory even as the conflict intensifies. Exit remains elusive as war enters second week.

outlook: CRITICAL IMMINENT — Global war probability SURGES to 83% (up 25 points from yesterday's 58%). Day 15 daily scan reveals systemic escalation across nearly all theaters. TWO TRACKS AT IMMINENT (100%): Iran conventional war and Israel-Lebanon. COUPLING CASCADE: Iran's imminent status amplifies Turkey (59, critical), Russia (38, critical), and Israel-Lebanon (100). Turkey and Russia both at critical creates feedback loop pushing Russia-Ukraine to 45. NEW ACTIVE ZONES: DPRK missile test pushes to 22 (elevated), Pakistan-Afghanistan jumps to 36 (critical) with new civilian casualties. China remains deterrent (18) but THAAD redeployment and warplane resumption signal watch status. NUCLEAR TRACK: Still de-escalating at 14% — the one positive. US deliberately calibrating strikes (Kharg Island military targets only, oil spared) to avoid triggering nuclear desperation. However, the conventional escalation trajectory is extremely dangerous. KEY RISKS: Iran retaliation for Kharg could be qualitatively different (parliament speaker promised 'new level'). Lebanon ground invasion appears days away. Turkey one intercept from Article 4. DPRK using distraction for testing. US air refueling fleet taking unsustainable losses. Oil above $100 with potential to spike higher. The next 48 hours are the most dangerous period of the conflict so far.
